response:
We are given the 4×4 matrix:

$$
A = \begin{bmatrix}
-5 & -5 & 5 & 1 \\
-3 & -3 & -4 & -1 \\
-4 & 1 & 1 & 4 \\
2 & 2 & 4 & 4
\end{bmatrix}
$$

We are asked to find the **determinant** of this matrix.

---

### Step 1: Use the Rule of Sarrus or cofactor expansion

For a 4×4 matrix, the determinant can be computed using cofactor expansion (along any row or column), or using row or column operations to simplify the matrix.

Let’s use **cofactor expansion along the first row**:

$$
\text{det}(A) = -5 \cdot \text{det}\begin{bmatrix}
-3 & -4 & -1 \\
1 & 1 & 4 \\
2 & 4 & 4
\end{bmatrix}
+ (-5) \cdot \text{det}\begin{bmatrix}
-3 & -4 & -1 \\
-4 & 1 & 4 \\
2 & 4 & 4
\end{bmatrix}
+ 5 \cdot \text{det}\begin{bmatrix}
-3 & -3 & -1 \\
-4 & 1 & 4 \\
2 & 2 & 4
\end{bmatrix}
+ 1 \cdot \text{det}\begin{bmatrix}
-3 & -3 & -4 \\
-4 & 1 & 1 \\
2 & 2 & 4
\end{bmatrix}
$$

---

### Step 2: Compute each 3×3 determinant

Let’s compute each of these 3×3 determinants one by one.

#### 1. First minor:

$$
\text{det}\begin{bmatrix}
-3 & -4 & -1 \\
1 & 1 & 4 \\
2 & 4 & 4
\end{bmatrix}
= (-3)(1 \cdot 4 - 4 \cdot 4) - (-4)(1 \cdot 4 - 4 \cdot 2) + (-1)(1 \cdot 4 - 1 \cdot 2)
$$

$$
= (-3)(4 - 16) + 4(4 - 8) - 1
